Video.summary: Daimlers and Morgans, and Citroens, oh my! Caffeine & Octane is known for having something for everyone. That reputation grows stronger as Bob Boston’s collection, on the lunatic fringe of automotive, rattles into an event; guest host Katherine Legge explains the competitor to her racing sponsor Acura, Porsche.
Video.title: Boston's (Un)Common Collection

Video.summary: Early spring in Jekyll Island, GA is something to behold. Add a few hundred of the country’s coolest rides for Caffeine & Octane at the Beach, and it’s a thing of beauty. Models that blasted the glass ceiling of expectation at the all makes and models show include a hand-built, twin-turbo Tucker, a one-off 1951 Kaiser Drag’N, and a Roadkill Custom known only as Blasphemi!
Video.title: C&O at the Beach

Video.summary: Caffeine & Octane is back at the beach on Jekyll Island with a focus on cars and families. Fathers & sons, with their barn-find Pilot House trucks and Plymouth Satellites, call cars the glue that keeps them bonded together with C&O being the best place to show them off.
Video.title: Kenny Rogers' Family Values

Video.summary: On this edition of Caffeine & Octane, we’ll go inside the head of Master Builder Bryan Fuller at the SEMA car show, the largest annual trade show in the country; SEMA always brings out the best in the automotive industry. From a jaw dropping Javelin to a multimillion-dollar Pagani, Brian will prove to you, what happens in Vegas, doesn’t always stay in Vegas! Ryan Hardwick guest hosts and ventures deep into the C&O lots to find his favorite ride of the day.
Video.title: SEMA: Inside Fuller's Head

Video.summary: Take apart and assemble a motor in less than 30 minutes? Sure, NASCAR pit crews can make the claim, but can a rag tag group of kids with names like Jelly Donut, Butter Bean, and Wonder Woman claim the pit fame? Host Rickey Gadson heads to the Sims Academy to settle a bet. This ultra fast half-hour edition of Caffeine & Octane also includes a builder’s take on an Ultra Four Unlimited Off-Road Racer; Ryan Hardwick joins the gang at Caffeine and Octane.
Video.title: Motor Masters

Video.summary: The team tears down Mike Hill's '70 Superbird and finishes up final touches on Chris Driscoll's '70 AAR Cuda. Daren confronts Mark about stealing parts from his car and Mark gets a rematch on Chris Driscoll's race track.
Video.title: AAR You Ready for This?

Video.summary: Ray finds a Gurney Eagle in a garage on Long Island. He wants to put the coveted machine back on the track at Indy to honor Dan Gurney. After intense restoration and scouring the globe for a Gurney-Westlake engine, the Eagle soars again.
Video.title: DAN GURNEY

Video.summary: Ray uncovers the infamous “Brawner Hawk” – from the Dean Van Lines team – that became the breakout car in the career of Mario Andretti. Ray enlists HOF mechanic Jim McGee to restore the vehicle and, along the way, discovers a replica with many of the car’s original parts. After an award winning stop at Concours Amelia Island, the car heads to the 100th running of Indy 500 where Mario Andretti himself gets behind the wheel.
Video.title: VINTAGE RACING

Video.summary: Dale Earnhardt Jr. wants Ray to help restore an El Camino, except add on a Laguna front end. It’s not a typical restoration, but it’s certainly a NASCAR performance-worthy restoration. Ray and the team build the car and then, in a bit of gamesmanship, challenge Kyle Tucker, owner of Detroit Speed, to a series of races against his award winning Camaro. But as always, Ray has an ace up his sleeve in Al Unser, Jr. as his driver.
Video.title: TWO JUNIORS

Video.summary: In the late 60s, Camaros, Mustangs, and Corvettes roared to the forefront of the muscle car world. When Ray gets offered a spot in a vintage race featuring these cars, he’s propelled to dive into their history and afforded a chance to get behind the wheel at the Brickyard. But because it is a pro-am race, Ray pulls in a partner – none other than Bill Elliott, aka “Awesome Bill from Dawsonville.”
Video.title: MACHINE MAGIC MARIO

Video.summary: Everyone has a car they fell in love with. Maybe it was the first? Maybe it was the hot rod the guy down the street owned? In Ray’s case, it was the ’58 Chevy he saw in the iconic coming of age car movie, American Graffiti. When Ray gets the chance to own the car after years and years of pursuit, he jumps on it. But restoring the car to its "movie state" proves to be a grueling task that goes way beyond a typical build. But when it's you first love, you can't let her down.
Video.title: HOLLYWOOD MAKEOVER

Video.summary: The premiere episode features a dive into the influence cars had on culture of California. From road racing in the 1940’s to the inception of the iconic Woody wagon on the California beach scene, automobiles undoubtedly shaped the West Coast attitude. Ray talks to The Beach Boys frontman Mike Love and surfing icon Laird Hamilton for a glimpse into the cars that are so connected to surfing culture.
Video.title: WOODIE WAGONS

Video.summary: Ray uncovers the secrets of America’s greatest racing sport in the foothills of North Carolina. When moonshiners were looking to outrun police officers, they would custom fit engines to race the back woods tracks. That sport eventually evolved into NASCAR and Ray talks to the early adopters of this national pastime.
Video.title: MOONSHINE

Video.summary: Imagination, ingenuity, and heartache join friends who set out with a simple goal of transforming a ‘35 Plymouth into a running taxi cab. Provenance and passion take center stage as families and friends strive for best in show. Host Rickey Gadson checks out perfect replicas of the original Shelby Cobras and takes Skip and Bryan on ‘borrowed’ taxi ride.
Video.title: Big Yellow Taxi

Video.summary: Call the Peach State Challengers a “car club” and you will quickly be corrected. The defenders of everything Dodge are “family” and they make no excuses for their loyalty to brand and brother. Spend a day at the track with these Challengers and find out what their version of the family photo looks like.
Video.title: Peach State Challengers

Video.summary: Former Jet Ski Champion Ryan Hardwick has succeeded in everything he’s touched in racing and business, so why not race Lamborghinis! Hardwick has found rapid success with his high tech training methods; he shares his secrets with host Rickey Gadson in this episode of C&O.
Video.title: Lambo Racer

Video.summary: Scott Borchetta, CEO and Founder of Big Machine Label Group, and his brother, Mark, love racing just as much as anyone. From Indy to Nashville, Ray finds he has a lot in common with the Borchetta brothers. This celebrity-filled episode features musicians Tim McGraw, Cassadee Pope and IndyCar driver Josef Newgarden.
Video.title: CARS AND MUSIC STARS

Video.summary: In 1964, the United States sent a star-studded race team to England to educate them on drag racing. On that team was Bob Keith and his Dos Palmas car. After being lost for over 40 years, Ray uncovers car and puts together a plan to reunite the two once more. The restoration of the car takes an emotional turn with the loss of a friend.
Video.title: DRAGSTER DIPLOMACY

Video.summary: Blues legend Joe Bonamassa is looking for a surprise gift for his parents. With his love of cars and guitars, Joe has to get Ray and Mr. H involved. From Mopar muscle to Mr. H’s man cave to a concert performance from Joe, this episode has a lot of Drive.
Video.title: SMOKIN JOE

Video.summary: In the early 1900s, race tracks built of wood sprang up across the country drawing crowds of 50,000 and more. Ray and Xtreme sports superstar Travis Pastrana break down the Battle on the Boards.
Video.title: BOARD TRACK BATTLES

Video.summary: Ray finds an old T-Bucket that made a 133 mph pass at the Bonneville Salt Flats in 1951. The team brings the Woody Lee T back to life and attempts to match its original speed.
Video.title: MODEL T

Video.summary: Holly asks Mark if she can work on cars. Mark Cain from Bad Boy Blasters brings the crew a huge gift. The '70 Barracuda Convertible is torn down and sent to the dipper, progress is made on the '70 Sunroof Challenger and Mark gets a surprise from one of his favorite actors.
Video.title: Go for It!

Video.summary: Mark installs the hood decal on the '70 Sunroof Challenger, Holly helps Josh disassemble the Barracuda suspension, Mark and Daren work on the 440 engine for the Challenger, and the team assembles one of the rarest Mopar options, the sunroof for the '70 Challenger.
Video.title: Get Your Hands Dirty

Video.summary: The team rushes to ready the '69 Dodge Daytona for Tom Partridge and his family's visit. The guys settle a leadership dispute the old fashioned way, the Graveyard Carz van gets a face lift, and Daren retaliates when Mark won't let him sign for deliveries.
Video.title: W-O-M-A-N, Mark Woman

Video.summary: Holly helps Mark on the '70 Challenger, Daren annoys everyone with his popcorn and causes Mark to damage a very rare car. Tony D'Agostino, from Tony's Mopar Parts, visits and lends a hand on the '70 sunroof Challenger. Mark lets Holly in on the big secret and Daren reveals a secret of his own.
Video.title: Pop Secrets

Video.summary: Mark gets a portable showroom, the '70 Hemi Charger is dissembled and the team finishes the 1970 Sunroof Challenger. Mark finally reveals his big secret project to the Graveyard Carz team and Royal makes a video tribute to the Sunroof Challenger.
Video.title: Let the Sunshine In

Video.summary: Mark and the ghouls build out the 440 Super Commando engine for their '70 Plymouth Superbird and install the suspension for the '69 Daytona Charger. Daren finally has enough of Mark's foolishness.
Video.title: The Royal Treatment

Video.summary: Body man Derrick becomes an official Graveyard Carz crew member and assists in the disassembly of the rare '67 Plymouth Hemi GTX Convertible. Josh and Daren play "the game" with Mark.
Video.title: The Grand Torino

Video.summary: Mark and the Ghoulz get a massive sheet-metal order from AMD for their '70 Plymouth Superbird. Meanwhile, the owners of the '72 Charger visit and talk about what made their car so special. Daren gets the offer of a lifetime on his '70 Challenger R/T.
Video.title: The Waltons

Video.summary: Matt and Bruno have a large Truck U checklist. They have to get a Bronco Project running again. Bruno has a racer friend stopping by who needs a hand and Matt shows us the new shop purchase.
Video.title: Truck U Checklist

Video.summary: Time to get low with a K5 Blazer. Matt and Bruno are going to C-notch the frame and and this 73’ blazer while still using the factory wheels and tires.
Video.title: K5 Blazer

Video.summary: Bruno takes a trip to San Antonio, Texas to meet the soldiers of Operation Comfort and see what makes their Bronco Weapons Carrier so special
Video.title: Operation Comfort

Video.summary: Bruno and Matt put together a Monster transmission from scratch and start building the tube chassis for the Rock Racer
Video.title: Monster in a Box

Video.summary: Not all trucks are perfect. Matt and Bruno dissect some common Ford problems in the engine and suspension.
Video.title: Ford Common Problems

Video.summary: Bruno and Matt improve the ride quality and tow capacity on our Ford Super Duty tow project by installing an adjustable airbag system.
Video.title: Air Bag It Part 1

Video.summary: We finish the Air Bag kit on our Super Duty tow project and put it to the test on the road.
Video.title: Air Bag It Part 2

Video.summary: Bruno and Matt install some upgrades on a 2010 Ford F 150 to make it more roadworthy
Video.title: Street Truck

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you how to remove bugs, polish exhaust tips and safetly remove dust
Video.title: Dust, Bugs and More

Video.summary: Nick Rutter shows you the process of applying a coating from claying, polishing, and maintenance
Video.title: Coating Your Car
